stories.headline: In shift, Trump praises Zelensky, will let Ukraine build Patriot missiles
stories.summary: It was a dramatic departure from Trump’s more acerbic tone toward Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, whom he once derided as ungrateful.

stories.headline: Trump grants Ukraine licence to produce Patriot missiles
stories.summary: US president agrees to longstanding demand from Kyiv to make its own PAC-3 interceptors

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is ‘over’ and warns of further strikes
stories.summary: Oil surges more than 8 per cent to exceed $80 a barrel after US president says negotiating with Tehran is a ‘waste of time’

stories.headline: US to give Ukraine licence to produce Patriot air-defence interceptors, Trump says
stories.summary: Donald Trump said the US will grant Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot interceptors after more than six months of lobbying from Kyiv.

stories.headline: Trump skips new Air Force One on return from Turkey NATO summit
stories.summary: Asked during a press conference whether the change was for security reasons related to Iran, Trump sidestepped the question by talking about previous alleged assassination attempts by Tehran.

stories.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

stories.headline: Trump takes aim at 'wasted cause' Spain and revives Greenland claim at Nato summit
stories.summary: The US president lashes out at fellow Nato member Spain, repeating an earlier threat to cut off trade relations.

stories.headline: EM Assets Slide as Trump Threatens Iran Strikes, New Blockade
stories.summary: Emerging-market assets sank Wednesday after the US renewed strikes on Iran and threatened to reimpose a blockade on its oil, pushing the price of crude further up and undermining the fragile ceasefire.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens More Iran Strikes
stories.summary: Leslie Vinjamuri, the President and CEO of the Chicago Council on Global Affairs joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why President Trump's latest Iran threats are aimed at gaining negotiating leverage rather than launching a full-scale war. stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
stories.summary: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.

stories.headline: Sabre-rattling to ‘tremendous love’: erratic Trump dominates final hours of Nato summit

stories.headline: 'We're gonna hit 'em hard tonight,' Trump says about further air strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Trump had said that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" after Washington launched strikes on the country overnight, to which Iran responded by targeting US bases in the Gulf.

stories.headline: Trump tempers fury with allies and ends 'tremendously successful' NATO summit on high note
stories.summary: European leaders showcased their surging defence spending at the summit as they looked to convince Trump they're making good on a pledge to ramp up budgets and take more responsibility for their own security.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: NATO pledges 70 billion euros for Ukraine as Trump praises peace ‘progress’
stories.summary: Trump says US will 'show' Ukraine how to produce Patriot missile systems during a friendly appearance with Zelenskyy.

stories.headline: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
stories.summary: Although the Patriots are highly effective against Russia's ballistic missiles, they also have lengthy production times.

stories.headline: Why the US and Iran are stuck in a cycle of tit-for-tat strikes

stories.headline: If Platner Exits Senate Race, the Maine Democratic Party Chair Could Help Pick the Nominee
stories.summary: Charles Dingman, chair of the Maine Democratic Party and a progressive, would play a key role in choosing the state’s Democratic Senate candidate if Graham Platner leaves the race.

stories.headline: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
stories.summary: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Ukraine intensifies attacks on Russian ships in Azov Sea
stories.summary: Eight small fuel tankers supplying Crimea were hit overnight, says Kyiv

stories.headline: Dollar Gets Safe-Haven Flows as US-Iran Ceasefire Under Threat
stories.summary: The dollar advanced for a second straight session as escalating fears of a return to full-scale war between the US and Iran prompted investors to seek shelter.

stories.headline: Democratic factions vie to pick Platner replacement before he leaves Maine race
stories.summary: Potential candidates and their backers moved quickly to gain an edge in the contest over who would replace Graham Platner if he exits the key Senate race.

stories.headline: War on Gaza draws large crowds to Khamenei’s funeral in Iraq
stories.summary: More than a million mourners packed streets in Najaf, Iraq for Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei’s funeral procession.

stories.headline: Pakistan military says 42 killed in fighter attacks in Balochistan
stories.summary: Military spokesman warns fighters 'we will chase you, we will hurt you' amid the spate of deadly ambushes.

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: A mountain of rubbish in Indonesia has been on fire for more than a week
stories.summary: Environmental activists say the blaze is a symptom of the country's growing waste crisis.

stories.headline: What's at stake in Philippines' vice-president impeachment trial?
stories.summary: The political deathmatch between the country's ruling dynasties enters a critical new theatre.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy seeks NATO support as Russia continues attacks
stories.summary: At the NATO summit, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had urged European allies to build missiles.

Planting the seeds of change: Qatar’s Food Security Strategy
stories.summary: In a country with more sand than soil, forging food security is not so straightforward. So, what does it take to grow resilience closer to home? Qatar in Motion explores how Qatar rewrote the rules of agriculture to reshape what is possible in the desert.

stories.headline: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer will convene around a dozen leaders to discuss the programme in Ankara.

stories.headline: US rare earths flow to Asia as domestic demand is slow to emerge
stories.summary: Miners backed by Trump administration sell to Japan and South Korea despite push to develop domestic supply chain

stories.headline: Torrid Pace for Deals: Paul Weiss's Veeraraghavan
stories.summary:
Krishna Veeraraghavan, global co-head of M&A at Paul Weiss, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." They discuss the current pace of M&A deals, capital coming back into public markets, and AI continuing to drive deal activity. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Top India Asset Manager Sets Price Range for $1.22 Billion IPO
stories.summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd.’s biggest investors are seeking to raise as much as 116.9 billion rupees ($1.22 billion) in what’s expected to be India’s first billion-dollar IPO of the year.

stories.headline: Los Angeles Hospital Blames Covid, Lender Dispute for Bankruptcy
stories.summary: Pacifica Hospital of the Valley, which serves poor and homeless residents in Los Angeles, filed for bankruptcy, blaming old financial problems linked to the Covid-19 pandemic and a more recent dispute with lenders.

stories.headline: Brazil Energy Firm Origem Is Said to Seek $2.5 Billion Valuation
stories.summary: Origem Energia SA, the Brazilian natural gas and oil company, is aiming for an enterprise value of $2 billion to $2.5 billion as it seeks strategic alternatives to raise capital, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: UK Carpet Firm Victoria Inks Deal With Left-Behind Creditors
stories.summary: UK carpet maker Victoria Plc and a group of bondholders left behind in an earlier debt deal have struck an agreement over a balance sheet restructuring.

stories.headline: Saudi Efforts to Revive Gulf Oil Loadings Run Into Buyer Caution
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s attempts to revive oil flows from its reopened Ras Tanura terminal on the Persian Gulf are running into caution from customers, who are reluctant to load there because of renewed threats to shipping, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Judge Hannah C. Dugan Faces Fine But No Prison Time
stories.summary: The former Wisconsin judge, Hannah C. Dugan, was accused of ushering an undocumented immigrant out a side door as federal agents waited for him near her Milwaukee courtroom.

IMF sharply downgrades Cambodia economic forecasts
Singapore hospital group makes market debut as population ages

stories.summary: Tough talks on the EU’s next long-term budget, Ukraine’s push for EU accession and skyrocketing fertiliser costs are putting European farmers under the political spotlight once more. Can the EU continue to appease them as it shifts its focus to defence and global trade? MEPs debate on The Ring.

stories.headline: EU faces tougher gas storage refill task as winter looms and high prices bite
stories.summary: EU gas storage was only 28 percent full at the start of the summer injection season, but energy regulators are confident in the system's resilience thanks to regasification capacity.

stories.headline: Oil Soars as Trump Says Iran Truce Over, More Strikes Ahead
stories.summary: Oil jumped to a two-week high after the US renewed attacks on Iran and President Donald Trump said an interim peace deal is over, heightening concerns about the free flow of crude from one of the world’s most important energy-producing regions.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
stories.summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

stories.headline: Zelensky presses Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president says "decisions for air defence" should be "one of the key outcomes" of this week's summit in Turkey.

stories.headline: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
stories.summary: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Jitters Spur Rally in Oil: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A flare-up in geopolitical risks sent stocks and bonds lower as oil jumped after President Donald Trump said a ceasefire with Iran may be over, adding the US would probably launch further strikes.

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Iran nuclear and military damage revealed after restricted satellite images released
stories.summary: Targets hit include ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.
